posts.name: Jeremy Holmes
posts.cooked:
It is always a pleasure to taste each year with Olivier Lamy. His wines are always so pure and possess great clarity. They generally give you a very good view into the nuance of a vintage. The ‘16’s from barrel show tremendous potential. Olivier noted that acidities were very similar to 2011.
2016 Hubert Lamy Bourgogne Blanc Les Chataigners: Very citrus, very pure and very direct.

2016 Hubert Lamy Saint-Aubin La Princée: The minerality is front and centre for now. Again, the fruit profile is of the citrus persuasion. It is very fine in the mouth with plenty of chewy dry extract on the back-end.

2016 Hubert Lamy Chassagne-Montrachet Le Concis du Champs: This is very much fruit driven, oodles of crammed with ripe peaches. It is round with good volume and a sappy finish.

2016 Hubert Lamy Puligny-Montrachet Les Tremblots: Very fine and minerally. Perfectly ripe white peach fruit. Compact and linear.

2016 Hubert Lamy Santenay 1er Cru Gravières: A wine of good shape. Fresh citrus and peach fruits are trimmed with floral spice.

2016 Hubert Lamy Saint-Aubin 1er Cru Clos de Meix: Really good phenolic grip here. It is linear, with chalky grip and a big lick of salty minerality. There’s excellent density and length.

2016 Hubert Lamy Saint-Aubin 1er Cru Derrière Chez Edouard: Engaging nose of lime and lemon fruits. It has great intensity of fruit at its heart and a line of piercing acidity. It has very good complexity and persistence and should be something special.
2016 Hubert Lamy Saint-Aubin 1er Cru Les Frionnes: The nose is all citrus blossom and lemon. It is dense and chalky with plenty of salinity to the finish.

2016 Hubert Lamy Saint-Aubin 1er Cru Les Murgers des Dents de Chien: Shy and demure for now. There’s some spice, excellent texture and great intensity and concentration. Awesome potential here.

2016 Hubert Lamy Saint-Aubin 1er Cru Clos de la Chateniere: a mélange of mineral and citrus. So intense, so linear, so long.

2016 Hubert Lamy Saint-Aubin 1er Cru En Remilly: The first impression is of sweet, ripe fruit. It is sappy and loaded with citrus. There’s plenty of mineral and spice and it really dries off on the finish. It is very chewy with great length.

2016 Hubert Lamy Chassagne-Montrachet 1er Cru Les Chaumées: Ripe, heady notes to the aroma. It is full and sappy, with dense orchard fruit flavour and excellent presence and persistence.

2016 Hubert Lamy Puligny-Montrachet Les Tremblots Cuvée Haute Densité: Serious intensity. All salt, lemon and chalk. Rigid spine. Fabulous length.

2016 Hubert Lamy Saint-Aubin 1er Cru Derrière Chez Edouard Cuvée Haute Densité: There’s a touch of reduction to the aroma. You get a whiff of toast, some flint and plenty of citrus. It is very fine in the mouth and builds through the palate. Great finesse and length.

2015 Hubert Lamy Criots-Bâtard-Montrachet, Grand Cu: Here you get delicacy with power. It is layered, spicy and possesses remarkable balance and poise. There’s pungent minerality and phenomenal length. Simply brilliant.

2015 Hubert Lamy Puligny-Montrachet Les Tremblots: Ripe peach fruit is trimmed with spice. It is full and shapely with a kiss of minerality to the finish.

2015 Hubert Lamy Saint-Aubin 1er Cru Clos de Meix: Complex notes of candied peel, peach, aniseed and freshly grated ginger. It is generous, layered and long.

2015 Hubert Lamy Saint-Aubin 1er Cru Derrière Chez Edouard: A delicate and fine nose of fragrant white peach, citrus blossom and aniseed. It has very good intensity and really builds through the palate. There’s a rigid mineral spine and excellent length.

2015 Hubert Lamy Saint-Aubin 1er Cru Les Frionnes: The nose is bright and punchy with whiffs of ginger and ripe orchard fruits. It is very mineral in the mouth and finishes with excellent freshness.

2015 Hubert Lamy Saint-Aubin 1er Cru Clos de la Chateniere: Complex aromatics of toast, citrus blossom and mineral. In the mouth the ripe and rich fruits are sweet and sappy. It finishes with saline minerality.

2015 Hubert Lamy Saint-Aubin 1er Cru En Remilly: Ripe orchard fruits are cut by a line of grapefruit acidity. It has good volume and plenty of dry extract. Persistence is excellent.

2015 Hubert Lamy Puligny-Montrachet Les Tremblots Cuvée Haute Densité: super intense, rich and powerful. Loaded with explosive white peach fruit. There’s a suggestion of aniseed and a squeeze of tart lemon to the finish.

2015 Hubert Lamy Saint-Aubin 1er Cru Derrière Chez Edouard Cuvée Haute Densité: Extremely tight, dense and chalky. There are powerful orchard fruits lurking below but for now they’re bound up in the wine’s considerable structure. It has so much intensity and will be majestic once it unfurls.

2008 Hubert Lamy Saint-Aubin 1er Cru Clos de la Chateniere: The fruit has a gentle exoticism to it. There’s some struck match and a line of spice. It is full and cut with grapefruit acidity. Drinking beautifully right now.

2008 Hubert Lamy Puligny-Montrachet Les Tremblots: Just taking on a hint of mushroom development. There’s pure white peach fruit and a touch of vanilla. It has good shape and a dry minerally finish.

2015 Hubert Lamy Saint-Aubin 1er Cru Derrière Chez Edouard: Sweet and luscious berry fruit overlay a bed of chalk.

posts.name: Мајкл Ристев
posts.cooked: any suggestion or idea of the aging potential for the haute densité bottlings?

posts.name: Jeremy Holmes
posts.cooked:
Hi Mајкл,

I haven’t had the Tremblot H.D with age on it but have had the Derrière Chez Edouard Cuvée Haute Densité a few times at around 7-8 years. The '09 for instance is drinking beautifully now but with plenty left in the tank. The '11 is still too tight.

Cheers
Jeremy

posts.cooked: thanks for the info. i have two bottles of the 2013 tremblots haute densité but have never tasted any of the HD wines before.
